---
title: "![AAU logo](images/AAU_GREEN_DARKTONE.png){width=5%} Earth Day 2025"
subtitle: "Climate Change Data Dashboards"
image: /images/earth_day_cc.png
---

# Schedule 

- 09:30-10:00 Welcome coffee and registration[^1].

- 10:00-10:30 **Uneven buffering of U.S. maize yields against extreme heat** by [*Marina Friedrich*](https://sites.google.com/view/mfriedrich), Assistant Professor, Vrije Universiteit Amsterdam.

- 10:30-11:00 **Learning from Earth’s climate history** by [Kathrine By Larsen](https://www.linkedin.com/in/kathrine-by-larsen/?originalSubdomain=dk), PhD Student in Climate Econometrics in the Department of Economics and Business Economics and CoRE, Aarhus University, Denmark.

- 11:00-11:10 Coffee break.

- 11:10-11:40 **Do Renewable Energy Subsidies Work?** by [Linde Kattenberg](https://sites.google.com/view/lindekattenberg), Postdoctoral Researcher in the Department of Economics and Business Economics and CoRE, Aarhus University, Denmark.

- 11:40-12:10 "**Spurious Relationships in Climate Data**" by [Daniel Ventosa-Santaulària](https://www.ventosa-santaularia.com/), Professor of Econometrics and Time Series Analysis at CIDE, Mexico City, Mexico.

- 12:10-12:20 Break.

- 12:20-12:30 Hackathon instructions and Q&A.

- 12:30-17:00 Hackathon with pizza drop around 13:00.

- 17:00-17:30 Evaluation of solutions and hackathon winner announcement[^1].

- 18:30 Event dinner for speakers.

[^1]: The research presentations are aimed at a general audience. *No previous experience on the topics is needed*. Presentations are 25 minutes + 5 minutes for questions.

[^2]: Prizes will be awarded to the best solutions.
